# Sub 8: Benchmarking gemma4 + context-window reuse research

## Parent Epic

#12065 — Sub 8 of 9. **NEW per operator-direct addition 2026-05-27 ~01:36Z** post-STEP_BACK. Parallel to Sub 1.

## Premise

Operator: *"we need benchmarking for gemma4 => creating context windows is the most expensive task. if there is any way to REUSE created context windows => big win."*

Each `SemanticGraphExtractor.executeTriVectorExtraction` invocation today opens a fresh gemma4 context for the LLM call (the OpenAI-compat surface via Ollama). With session payloads up to 256K tokens + 10 sessions per REM cycle, the cumulative context-creation cost may dominate the actual inference cost. If Ollama / OpenAI-compat backend supports context-cache reuse (KV-cache persistence across calls within the same session OR shared system-prompt cache across calls), we could reduce per-cycle wall-clock from MINUTES × N → MINUTES + tiny-deltas.

## Prescription

Two parts:

**Part A — Benchmarking baseline:**
- Measure wall-clock + token-count per phase of `executeTriVectorExtraction`:
  - Time to first token (TTFT) — proxy for context-creation cost
  - Time to last token (TTLT) — proxy for inference cost
  - Tokens-per-second during generation
- Capture for sessions of different sizes: small (~5K tokens), medium (~30K), large (~100K), max-band (~200K)
- Run via `ai:run-sandman` against fresh + warmed states; isolate the per-call context-creation overhead
- Document baseline in `learn/agentos/gemma4-rem-benchmark.md` or similar

**Part B — Context-window-reuse research:**
- Investigate Ollama API: does `keep_alive` parameter enable context reuse across calls? (Ollama docs: `keep_alive` controls how long the model stays in memory after a request; affects KV-cache retention)
- Investigate OpenAI-compat API: any reuse semantics in the proxy surface?
- Test empirically: 2 back-to-back Tri-Vector calls — does TTFT drop on call 2?
- Document findings: if reuse works, propose Sub 7 chunking enhancement to keep chunks under one `keep_alive` window
- If reuse NOT possible at Ollama layer, propose alternative: longer-running gemma server process owning the context

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: Benchmark suite lands (script at `ai/scripts/benchmark/gemma4-rem-benchmark.mjs` or equivalent) — measures TTFT/TTLT/tps per session size
- [ ] AC2: Baseline measurement document published at `learn/agentos/gemma4-rem-benchmark.md` — captures pre-optimization numbers per the 4 size buckets
- [ ] AC3: Ollama `keep_alive` + KV-cache reuse investigation documented — empirical V-B-A of context-reuse capability
- [ ] AC4: If reuse works: integration recommendation for Sub 3 (`executeRemCycle` configures `keep_alive` for batch processing) + Sub 7 (chunk batching within one keep-alive window)
- [ ] AC5: If reuse doesn't work at Ollama layer: alternative documented (e.g., longer-running gemma server lifecycle owned by orchestrator)
- [ ] AC6: Cost-asymmetry quantification: fresh-context vs reused-context per invocation (expected: 5-20× speedup if reuse works)
- [ ] AC7: Benchmark integration with Sub 7's chunking strategy — verify 2-pass + reduce cost-asymmetry is acceptable (2-3× single-pass cost, NOT 10-20×)

## Avoided Traps

- ❌ Skip baseline measurement before proposing optimization — operator-explicit "benchmarking" framing requires empirical numbers, not theoretical claims
- ❌ Assume KV-cache reuse works without V-B-A — Ollama documentation is ambiguous; needs empirical test
- ❌ Optimize for gemma4 specifically without abstraction — keep findings provider-agnostic where possible (operator may switch to gemma-5 / qwen / etc.)

## Related

- Epic #12065
- Operator directive 2026-05-27 ~01:36Z: *"we need benchmarking for gemma4 => creating context windows is the most expensive task."*
- Discussion #12062 §2.4.1 (cap-raise hot-fix enables benchmarking with realistic payload sizes)
- Sub 3 (consumer integration if reuse works), Sub 7 (chunk-batching enhancement)
- ADR 0014 cloud-deployment-topology (cloud deployments use different gemma server; reuse findings may differ)

- 2026-05-27T02:44:38Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `6694bb9` - "fix(ai): address PR #12076 cycle-1 review — graph provider routing + percentile + keep_alive prose (#12074)

Addresses @neo-gpt cycle-1 CHANGES_REQUESTED on PR #12076 (3 required actions):

1. **Provider routing bug** — scripts passed `aiConfig.modelProvider` to
   `buildGraphProvider`, but with PR #12061 landing the chat-vs-graph
   provider axis split, default `modelProvider: gemini` causes
   `buildGraphProvider: unsupported modelProvider 'gemini'` at preflight
   before any measurement. Fix: inline the same resolution shape as
   PR #12061's `resolveGraphModelProvider` (`aiConfig.graphProvider` with
   fallback to `'openAiCompatible'` unless `modelProvider === 'ollama'`).
   TODO post-#12061-merge: replace inline logic with shared helper import.

   No-LLM smoke verified:
       node ai/scripts/benchmark/gemma4-rem-benchmark.mjs --size small \
           --iterations 0 --warmup 0 --output /tmp/gemma4-smoke.json
   now resolves `Graph provider: openAiCompatible (chat modelProvider: gemini)`
   and writes the empty-runs JSON cleanly. Same fix applied to
   keep-alive-probe.mjs.

   Result metadata now exposes both axes: `graphProvider` (the routing
   axis) + `chatModelProvider` (the orthogonal generic axis).

2. **Percentile off-by-one** — `Math.floor(p * N)` returned the wrong rank
   for NIST nearest-rank semantics. For [1..100] and p=0.95: Math.floor
   gives 95 (sorted[95] = 96), but NIST ordinal-rank = ceil(p * N) gives
   95 (sorted[94] = 95). Fixed to `Math.ceil(p * N) - 1` with clamp via
   `Math.max(0, ...)` for the p=0 edge case. JSDoc expanded with explicit
   NIST formula citation. Test corrected to assert 95 (not 96) for p95
   of [1..100]. Other test cases (p50/p=0/p=1/single-element) unchanged
   — they already passed both formulas.

3. **keep_alive prose drift** — earlier prose said both Ollama.stream()
   AND OpenAiCompatible.stream() "drop" keep_alive. Mechanically broad:
   `OpenAiCompatible.stream()` uses `preparePayload()` which
   `Object.assign(payload, clonedOptions)` — so caller-supplied keep_alive
   IS mechanically propagated. The unverified residual is whether the
   deployed server (LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, Ollama-`/v1/...`) HONORS
   the non-standard `keep_alive` extension. Updated:
   - `learn/agentos/gemma4-rem-benchmark.md` table rows for Ollama.stream
     and OpenAiCompatible.stream
   - `keep-alive-probe.mjs` @summary header

Test evidence:
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/benchmark/stats.spec.mjs`
  → 16/16 passed (570ms)
- `node ai/scripts/benchmark/gemma4-rem-benchmark.mjs --help` clean
- `node ai/scripts/benchmark/keep-alive-probe.mjs --help` clean
- `node ai/scripts/benchmark/gemma4-rem-benchmark.mjs --size small
   --iterations 0 --warmup 0 --output /tmp/gemma4-smoke.json` clean
  (no preflight provider-dispatch failure)"
- 2026-05-27T03:00:02Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `1127025` - "fix(ai): narrow keep-alive probe scope — native Ollama early-exit per #12076 cycle-2 review (#12074)

Addresses @neo-gpt cycle-2 CHANGES_REQUESTED on PR #12076 (1 of 2 required actions; body update follows):

V-B-A on Ollama.preparePayload() lines 91-92: arbitrary remaining
`options` are nested under `payload.options`, NOT top-level. Native
Ollama `/api/chat` reads `keep_alive` at TOP LEVEL of the request body,
so caller-supplied `keep_alive` via `provider.stream(messages,
{keep_alive})` becomes `payload.options.keep_alive` and is silently
ignored by Ollama. The probe's `keep_alive=0` control comparison would
be meaningless for the native Ollama path.

OpenAI-compatible providers are fine: `OpenAiCompatible.preparePayload()`
line 105 uses `Object.assign(payload, clonedOptions)`, so caller-
supplied `keep_alive` IS top-level in the JSON. The remaining V-B-A
target for OpenAI-compat is whether the deployed server HONORS the
non-standard `keep_alive` extension — that's what this probe still
characterizes.

Fix: early-exit on native Ollama with explicit message naming the
preparePayload nesting + the workaround (rely on `Ollama.generate()`
callsites which write `keep_alive` top-level at line 108). Follow-up
SDK fix to patch `Ollama.stream()` for top-level `keep_alive` injection
is tracked separately (out of scope for this benchmark PR).

Doc updates (`learn/agentos/gemma4-rem-benchmark.md`):
- Substrate V-B-A findings table now anchors `Ollama.mjs:91-92`
  (preparePayload nesting) instead of `Ollama.mjs:267-276` (stream() —
  not the actual source of the keep_alive issue).
- Interpretation matrix scoped to "OpenAI-compatible path only" with
  cross-reference to the native Ollama early-exit rationale.
- Implication paragraph narrowed: probe characterizes OpenAI-compat
  server-honor on the streaming path; native Ollama streaming requires
  SDK patch.

Probe @summary header updated to match (mirror of the doc V-B-A table
rows for in-file substrate consultation).

Empirical sanity-check (against operator's live LM Studio gemma-4-31b-it
on http://127.0.0.1:1234, graphProvider=openAiCompatible default):
- `--mode reuse`: TTFT call-1 2520ms → call-2 235ms (91% faster). Probe
  works; KV-cache reuse appears ACTIVE on the operator's deployed
  OpenAI-compat server. (Not committing this number to the doc —
  Phase-2 baselines remain operator-owned per design.)"
- 2026-05-27T03:04:31Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #12080
- 2026-05-27T05:43:54Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #12083
- 2026-05-27T12:14:08Z @tobiu referenced in commit `394c531` - "feat(ai): gemma4 REM-pipeline benchmark harness + keep_alive probe (#12074) (#12076)

* feat(ai): add gemma4 REM-pipeline benchmark harness + keep_alive probe (#12074)

Phase 1 of Epic #12065 Sub 8 — code-only ship; Phase 2 baseline numbers
land after operator runs on gemma4-equipped host.

Substrate added:

- `ai/scripts/benchmark/gemma4-rem-benchmark.mjs` — per-bucket cost
  characterization. Streams synthetic prompts at 4 size buckets
  (~5K / ~30K / ~100K / ~200K tokens) and records TTFT / TTLT / tps per
  call. Routes via `buildGraphProvider` so it mirrors the production
  SemanticGraphExtractor path, not a parallel one.

- `ai/scripts/benchmark/keep-alive-probe.mjs` — empirical V-B-A of provider
  KV-cache reuse via the `keep_alive` parameter. Two back-to-back identical
  calls; compares TTFT delta to decide whether reuse is active and whether
  `keep_alive` is the controlling knob.

- `ai/scripts/benchmark/helpers/stats.mjs` — pure median / percentile /
  summarize helpers. Lifted out of the harness so the math is provably
  correct independent of (LLM-dependent) measurement loops.

- `learn/agentos/gemma4-rem-benchmark.md` — measurement protocol +
  baseline-table-to-be-filled + integration recommendations decision tree
  for Sub 3 + Sub 7.

- `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/benchmark/stats.spec.mjs` — 16-case unit
  coverage for stats helpers (odd/even/empty/non-array median; nearest-rank
  p95 boundary cases; error-run filtering; n<5 omits p95).

- `package.json` — `ai:benchmark-gemma4` + `ai:probe-keep-alive` aliases.

Substrate V-B-A finding banked in doc + commit body, since the operator
likely doesn't know: `ai/provider/Ollama.mjs:108` hardcodes
`keep_alive: "1h"` in `generate()` ONLY. Both `Ollama.stream()` AND
`OpenAiCompatible.stream()` drop the param — if operator runs gemma via
the openAiCompatible host (current config default), the production
streaming path may silently lose KV-cache reuse. Probe will confirm.

Self-correction lesson: V-B-A present substrate BEFORE lane-claim on any
ticket, including tickets I authored myself at graduation time. Closing
#12071 from the same Epic for exactly this reason — its prescription was
duplicating existing `ai/scripts/runners/runGoldenPath.mjs`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply-suppressed>

* fix(ai): address PR #12076 cycle-1 review — graph provider routing + percentile + keep_alive prose (#12074)

Addresses @neo-gpt cycle-1 CHANGES_REQUESTED on PR #12076 (3 required actions):

1. **Provider routing bug** — scripts passed `aiConfig.modelProvider` to
   `buildGraphProvider`, but with PR #12061 landing the chat-vs-graph
   provider axis split, default `modelProvider: gemini` causes
   `buildGraphProvider: unsupported modelProvider 'gemini'` at preflight
   before any measurement. Fix: inline the same resolution shape as
   PR #12061's `resolveGraphModelProvider` (`aiConfig.graphProvider` with
   fallback to `'openAiCompatible'` unless `modelProvider === 'ollama'`).
   TODO post-#12061-merge: replace inline logic with shared helper import.

   No-LLM smoke verified:
       node ai/scripts/benchmark/gemma4-rem-benchmark.mjs --size small \
           --iterations 0 --warmup 0 --output /tmp/gemma4-smoke.json
   now resolves `Graph provider: openAiCompatible (chat modelProvider: gemini)`
   and writes the empty-runs JSON cleanly. Same fix applied to
   keep-alive-probe.mjs.

   Result metadata now exposes both axes: `graphProvider` (the routing
   axis) + `chatModelProvider` (the orthogonal generic axis).

2. **Percentile off-by-one** — `Math.floor(p * N)` returned the wrong rank
   for NIST nearest-rank semantics. For [1..100] and p=0.95: Math.floor
   gives 95 (sorted[95] = 96), but NIST ordinal-rank = ceil(p * N) gives
   95 (sorted[94] = 95). Fixed to `Math.ceil(p * N) - 1` with clamp via
   `Math.max(0, ...)` for the p=0 edge case. JSDoc expanded with explicit
   NIST formula citation. Test corrected to assert 95 (not 96) for p95
   of [1..100]. Other test cases (p50/p=0/p=1/single-element) unchanged
   — they already passed both formulas.

3. **keep_alive prose drift** — earlier prose said both Ollama.stream()
   AND OpenAiCompatible.stream() "drop" keep_alive. Mechanically broad:
   `OpenAiCompatible.stream()` uses `preparePayload()` which
   `Object.assign(payload, clonedOptions)` — so caller-supplied keep_alive
   IS mechanically propagated. The unverified residual is whether the
   deployed server (LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, Ollama-`/v1/...`) HONORS
   the non-standard `keep_alive` extension. Updated:
   - `learn/agentos/gemma4-rem-benchmark.md` table rows for Ollama.stream
     and OpenAiCompatible.stream
   - `keep-alive-probe.mjs` @summary header

Test evidence:
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/benchmark/stats.spec.mjs`
  → 16/16 passed (570ms)
- `node ai/scripts/benchmark/gemma4-rem-benchmark.mjs --help` clean
- `node ai/scripts/benchmark/keep-alive-probe.mjs --help` clean
- `node ai/scripts/benchmark/gemma4-rem-benchmark.mjs --size small
   --iterations 0 --warmup 0 --output /tmp/gemma4-smoke.json` clean
  (no preflight provider-dispatch failure)

* fix(ai): narrow keep-alive probe scope — native Ollama early-exit per #12076 cycle-2 review (#12074)

Addresses @neo-gpt cycle-2 CHANGES_REQUESTED on PR #12076 (1 of 2 required actions; body update follows):

V-B-A on Ollama.preparePayload() lines 91-92: arbitrary remaining
`options` are nested under `payload.options`, NOT top-level. Native
Ollama `/api/chat` reads `keep_alive` at TOP LEVEL of the request body,
so caller-supplied `keep_alive` via `provider.stream(messages,
{keep_alive})` becomes `payload.options.keep_alive` and is silently
ignored by Ollama. The probe's `keep_alive=0` control comparison would
be meaningless for the native Ollama path.

OpenAI-compatible providers are fine: `OpenAiCompatible.preparePayload()`
line 105 uses `Object.assign(payload, clonedOptions)`, so caller-
supplied `keep_alive` IS top-level in the JSON. The remaining V-B-A
target for OpenAI-compat is whether the deployed server HONORS the
non-standard `keep_alive` extension — that's what this probe still
characterizes.

Fix: early-exit on native Ollama with explicit message naming the
preparePayload nesting + the workaround (rely on `Ollama.generate()`
callsites which write `keep_alive` top-level at line 108). Follow-up
SDK fix to patch `Ollama.stream()` for top-level `keep_alive` injection
is tracked separately (out of scope for this benchmark PR).

Doc updates (`learn/agentos/gemma4-rem-benchmark.md`):
- Substrate V-B-A findings table now anchors `Ollama.mjs:91-92`
  (preparePayload nesting) instead of `Ollama.mjs:267-276` (stream() —
  not the actual source of the keep_alive issue).
- Interpretation matrix scoped to "OpenAI-compatible path only" with
  cross-reference to the native Ollama early-exit rationale.
- Implication paragraph narrowed: probe characterizes OpenAI-compat
  server-honor on the streaming path; native Ollama streaming requires
  SDK patch.

Probe @summary header updated to match (mirror of the doc V-B-A table
rows for in-file substrate consultation).

Empirical sanity-check (against operator's live LM Studio gemma-4-31b-it
on http://127.0.0.1:1234, graphProvider=openAiCompatible default):
- `--mode reuse`: TTFT call-1 2520ms → call-2 235ms (91% faster). Probe
  works; KV-cache reuse appears ACTIVE on the operator's deployed
  OpenAI-compat server. (Not committing this number to the doc —
  Phase-2 baselines remain operator-owned per design.)
